name: Product Research Team
description: Proposal template for in-depth product research, customer needs, solution framing, and practical product direction.
author: ClawMax Team

communities.name: Product Research
communities.description: Shared coordination space for customer and product research.

groups.name: Users
groups.description: Customer segments, needs, pains, and unmet jobs.

groups.name: Product
groups.description: Product framing, use cases, and opportunity areas.

groups.name: Status
groups.description: Current scope, high-signal findings, and next decisions.

agents.name: Research Director
agents.role: Research lead — sets the question, decision lens, and final product recommendation.

agents.name: Customer Researcher
agents.role: Customer specialist — maps pains, motivations, and usage patterns for target users.

agents.name: Product Strategist
agents.role: Strategy specialist — turns user needs into product directions, bets, and framing.

agents.name: Insight Editor
agents.role: Insight specialist — distills raw notes into crisp findings and decision-ready output.

workflows.name: Product Research Kickoff
workflows.content:
# Product Research Kickoff

You are the Research Director.

## Project Configuration
> **Customize these before applying:**

- **Product idea or area:** [idea]
- **Target user or segment:** [segment]
- **Research lens:** [problems, workflows, willingness to pay, adoption, custom]
- **Decision needed:** [go/no-go, scope, roadmap, positioning, custom]
- **Output artifact:** [research memo, PRD input, product brief]

## Your Tasks
1. Restate the product area, user, and decision needed
2. Ask Customer Researchers for the main user pains and jobs
3. Ask Product Strategist for the strongest opportunity framing
4. Ask Insight Editor for the final synthesis format
5. Post the research plan to Status

workflows.name: User Needs Sweep
workflows.description: Map the strongest user pains, jobs, motivations, and unmet needs for the target segment.
workflows.content:
# User Needs Sweep

1. Review the target user or segment defined in kickoff and restate the core context
2. Identify the most important pains, jobs-to-be-done, motivations, and adoption blockers for that segment
3. Distinguish well-supported observations from assumptions that still need validation
4. Organize findings into clear themes the product team can act on
5. Post the user-needs sweep to Users and a short status note to Status

workflows.name: Opportunity Framing Review
workflows.description: Turn the user needs into product directions, bets, and framing options.
workflows.content:
# Opportunity Framing Review

1. Review the product area, decision needed, and user-needs context from kickoff
2. Translate the strongest user needs into product directions, feature bets, and positioning options
3. Call out the most promising opportunities, the riskiest assumptions, and the likely tradeoffs
4. Recommend which directions deserve immediate validation versus deeper research
5. Post the opportunity framing review to Product and a short status note to Status

workflows.name: Product Findings Synthesis
workflows.content:
# Product Findings Synthesis

1. Collect the strongest customer and product observations
2. Turn them into a concise findings memo with tensions and recommendations
3. Call out what still needs validation
4. Post the final findings to Findings and the summary to Status
